mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: CLT: Can't Lose Track (of the mean's distribution becoming normal as sample size increases)

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: The sampling distribution describes the probability distribution of a statistic (like the mean) calculated from multiple random samples of a population.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The Central Limit Theorem (CLT) states that the sampling distribution of the sample mean will be approximately normal, regardless of the population's distribution, if the sample size is sufficiently large.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: The standard deviation of the sampling distribution of the mean is called the standard error, calculated as population standard deviation (σ) divided by the square root of the sample size (n).
